title: thelypteris subappendiculata
intro: Thelypteris subappendiculata, also known as the appendicular fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Thelypteris subappendiculata include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be applied topically to the affected area or ingested to promote overall well-being.
